title: spergula marginata var. pycnorrhiza
intro: Spergula marginata var. pycnorrhiza, also known as corn spurry or corn cleaver, is a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It is a small annual herb with medicinal properties, particularly in its use as a diuretic and anti-inflammatory agent. The plant has been traditionally used to treat a range of health issues, including kidney stones, arthritis, and skin conditions, due to its high concentration of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. Spergula marginata var. pycnorrhiza is often prepared in the form of teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
